content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The children ___ over the puddles on the way to school.
distractor_rationale: floated means to move gently on water or air — a person cannot float over a puddle on the ground
landed means to come down after being in the air — it cannot describe the action of going over puddles on foot
escaped means to get away from a dangerous situation — there is no danger or confinement described here

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She ___ the stairs slowly because her legs were tired after the long walk.
distractor_rationale: crossed means to go from one side to the other of a flat surface — you cross a road or a river, not stairs
followed means to move behind someone — there is no person being followed in this sentence
drove means to operate a vehicle — you cannot drive stairs

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The pilot ___ the plane safely at the airport after a six-hour journey.
distractor_rationale: floated means to move slowly through the air or water without control — a pilot does not float a plane
entered means to go into a place — you enter a building, not a plane onto a runway
followed means to move behind someone — there is no person or object being followed here

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Mark was late for his meeting, so he ___ down the corridor and knocked on the door.
distractor_rationale: floated means to move gently and slowly — this contradicts being late and needing to move quickly
climbed means to go upward using hands or feet — a corridor is flat, so climbing makes no sense here
flew means to move through the air — a person cannot literally fly down a corridor

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The dog ___ its owner all the way from the park to the front door of the house.
distractor_rationale: escaped means to get away from a place or person — the dog is moving toward its owner, not away
entered means to go into a place — the dog is described moving along a route, not going inside something
arrived means to reach a destination — arrived is intransitive and cannot take 'its owner' as a direct object

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The small boat ___ gently on the calm lake while the passengers relaxed inside.
distractor_rationale: landed means to come down and stop on a surface — a boat already on water does not land
hurried means to move quickly — this contradicts the calm, relaxed scene described in the sentence
crossed means to go from one side to the other — the sentence describes a gentle, stationary drift, not a purposeful crossing

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom ___ his car into the garage and turned off the engine before going inside.
distractor_rationale: followed means to move behind someone — there is no person or object being followed into the garage
entered means to go into a place on foot — you enter a building yourself, but you drive a car into a garage
crossed means to go from one side to the other of something — you cross a road or bridge, not drive into a garage

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The elderly man ___ the busy road carefully, waiting for the traffic lights to turn green before he stepped off the pavement.
distractor_rationale: entered means to go into an enclosed space — you enter a building or room, not a road
climbed means to go upward — a road is flat, so climbing it is nonsensical in this context
escaped means to get away from a place of danger or confinement — the man is making a planned, careful movement, not fleeing

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The cat managed to ___ from the garden by squeezing through a small gap in the fence while no one was watching.
distractor_rationale: arrive means to reach a destination — the cat is leaving the garden, not reaching one
float means to move gently on air or water — a cat squeezing through a fence gap cannot float
land means to come down after being in the air — the cat is moving horizontally through a fence, not descending from above

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After a long flight from abroad, Lisa was relieved to finally ___ at her home city's airport and see her family waiting for her.
distractor_rationale: escape means to get away from a dangerous or confined place — Lisa is completing a journey, not fleeing from anything
enter means to go into an enclosed space — you arrive at an airport, not enter it in the context of completing a journey from abroad
fall means to drop downward unintentionally — this contradicts the positive, relieved tone and the planned end of a journey

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The children ___ over the puddle on the pavement so their shoes stayed dry.
distractor_rationale: floated means to move on water or air without effort — a person cannot float over a puddle on the ground
climbed means to go up a surface such as a ladder or hill — a puddle on flat ground cannot be climbed
drove refers to operating a vehicle — children on foot cannot drive over a puddle

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria ___ the busy road carefully at the traffic lights before walking to the market.
distractor_rationale: entered means to go inside a place such as a building — a road is not entered, it is crossed
followed means to go behind someone or something — Maria is moving across the road, not behind another person
landed means to come down from the air onto a surface — Maria is on foot on the ground, not descending from above

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom had to ___ to the bus stop because he was already five minutes late for work.
distractor_rationale: float describes moving gently on water or air — a person rushing to a bus stop on foot cannot float
land means to arrive from the air onto a surface — Tom is already on the ground and moving along a street
climb means to go upward on a surface — a bus stop on a flat pavement is not something that is climbed to

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The pilot announced that the plane would ___ at the airport in about twenty minutes, so passengers should fasten their seatbelts.
distractor_rationale: arrive describes reaching a destination in general and cannot describe the physical act of a plane touching down on a runway — 'the plane arrives' is used for schedules, not the physical landing action described here with seatbelt instruction
enter means to go inside a place — a plane does not enter an airport in the way passengers do; it lands on a runway
escape means to get away from a dangerous or confined situation — landing safely at an airport is the opposite of escaping

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Anna had to ___ the locked back door of the building after she realised the main entrance had closed for the night.
distractor_rationale: enter means to go into a place — Anna is already inside and needs to get out through the back door, not go in
follow means to move behind someone — there is no person or route mentioned that Anna is moving behind
float means to move without effort on water or air — a person cannot float through a locked door in a building

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The children ___ over the puddle on the path so their shoes stayed dry.
distractor_rationale: floated means to move on air or water without effort, which a person cannot do over a puddle on a path
landed means to come down after being in the air, which is the end of a jump, not the act of crossing a puddle
escaped means to get away from danger or confinement, which contradicts the ordinary outdoor scene described

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria was late for the bus, so she ___ all the way to the stop.
distractor_rationale: floated means to drift slowly without effort, which is the opposite of moving quickly to catch a bus
climbed means to go up a surface using hands and feet, which cannot describe running to a bus stop on flat ground
flew means to move through the air, which a person cannot literally do on the way to a bus stop

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom ___ the heavy box and it hit the floor with a loud bang.
distractor_rationale: followed means to go behind someone or something, which cannot describe a box hitting the floor
entered means to go into a place, which cannot describe what happened to the box
crossed means to go from one side to the other, which does not explain the box hitting the floor

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The plane ___ safely at the airport after a three-hour flight from the coast.
distractor_rationale: arrived can describe reaching a destination but does not collocate specifically with a plane touching down on a runway, making it imprecise and technically wrong in aviation context
entered means to go into an enclosed space and cannot describe a plane touching down on a runway
fell implies an uncontrolled downward movement, which contradicts the word 'safely' in the sentence

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: David ___ the busy road carefully after checking both ways for traffic.
distractor_rationale: followed means to go behind someone or something, which does not describe moving from one side of a road to the other
entered means to go inside a place, and a road is not an enclosed space one can enter
climbed means to go upward using physical effort, which cannot describe walking across a flat road

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The dog ___ its owner all the way home from the park without stopping once.
distractor_rationale: crossed means to go from one side to the other, not to move behind someone over a long distance
escaped means to get away from a place or person, which is the opposite of staying close to the owner
drove means to operate a vehicle, and a dog cannot drive

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Sara ___ her car to the supermarket every Saturday morning to do the weekly shop.
distractor_rationale: flies means to travel through the air, which is impossible for a car on a road to a supermarket
floats means to drift on water or air without effort, which cannot describe a car moving on a road
climbs means to go upward on a surface, which cannot describe a car travelling to a supermarket

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The prisoner managed to ___ from the building through a small window at the back.
distractor_rationale: arrive means to reach a destination, which is the opposite of leaving a place through a window
enter means to go into a place, which contradicts moving out through a window to get away
land means to come down after being in the air, which does not describe getting out of a building through a window

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: A large piece of wood was ___ on the surface of the river after the storm broke the old bridge.
distractor_rationale: flying means moving through the air under power, which cannot describe a piece of wood resting on a river's surface
falling means moving downward quickly due to gravity, which contradicts the wood being on the surface of the water
climbing means moving upward on a surface, which cannot describe a piece of wood drifting on a river

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Anna had to ___ the steep hill behind the village to reach the viewpoint at the top.
distractor_rationale: cross means to go from one side to the other on a flat surface, which does not describe the upward effort needed to reach the top of a steep hill
enter means to go into an enclosed space, and a hill is not an enclosed space one can enter
follow means to go behind someone or something, and there is no person or path mentioned that Anna is going behind

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ of the plane was smooth and on time. (LAND)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The children were very ___ when they heard the school bus was late. (HURRY)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ of the river took longer than we expected because the current was very strong. (CROSS)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After a long delay at the airport, the passengers were relieved when the ___ was finally announced. (ARRIVE)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The mountain ___ lasted three days and required the team to carry all their equipment on their backs. (CLIMB)
